decision_commentary: Client communication log captures 4 events: proposal delivery (instagram_dm), proposal accepted, redesign notice, and redesign accepted. Project name is referenced in all entries. Accuracy scored 5: redesign_notice correctly sent at 14:45 and accepted at 15:30 — timeline consistent with DG-09 reject (14:00) and redesign scope completion. Channel scored 5: instagram_dm matches inbound channel for this client. Clarity scored 4: communications are summarized rather than containing full message bodies — one follow-up would be needed to see exact wording. Brand_voice scored 4: subject lines are bilingual (Spanish proposal title) which is appropriate, but full message bodies are not present to fully evaluate warmth. Note: redesign_notice is timestamped 14:45 but proposal_delivery is at 16:30 — this is chronologically inconsistent (redesign happened before proposal was sent per timestamps). State_sync scored 3 per simulated run note.
gap_note: Timestamp inconsistency: proposal_delivery at 16:30 appears after redesign_notice at 14:45 and redesign_accepted at 15:30. In a real project, proposal delivery would precede the budget mismatch redesign loop. Likely a sequencing artifact of the simulated run.
